english: pa to receive a letter; yi-ge (yi ge) sleb (sleb) a letter arrives; yi-ger (yi ger) ₒbri-ba ('bri ba) Dzl., ₒgod-pa ('god pa) to compose, to pen down; yi-ger (yi ger) ₒbrir ('brir) ₒjug-pa ('jug pa) to get copied; yi-ger (yi ger) bris (bris) ₒjog-pa ('jog pa) literis mandatum deponere; skú-yig (sku yig) letter, circular epistle; k'á-yig (kha yig) v. above; čád-yig (chad yig) contract, bargain; ₒčáms-yig ('chams yig) dancing-book, rules relating to religious dances; ₒčól-yig ('chol yig) letter of recommendation Cs.; rtágs-yig (rtags yig) 1. stamp, signature Cs. 2. certificate, credentials W.; gnás-yig (gnas yig) description of a place; spríṅs- (springs) or ₒprín-yig ('phrin yig) = skúr-yig (skur yig); bú-yig (bu yig) 1. copy. 2. commentary, opp. to má-yig (ma yig) 1. original, first copy; 2. text Cs.; ₒdzín-yig ('dzin yig) = rtágs-yig (rtags yig) 2 W.; żú-yig (zhu yig) memorial, petition; lán-yig (lan yig) letter in answer, reply; lám-yig (lam yig) 1. hand-book, road-book, guide, ṡám-bha-lai (sham bha la'i) lam-yig (lam yig) description of the road to Sambhala (a fantastical book). 2. itinerary, travelling-journal (?). 3. pass-port Cs.

(from etymol. subtility written also yid-tags (yid tags) or yid-btags (yid btags)), प्रेत, the fifth class of beings of Buddhist cosmography, condemned in a fore-hell to suffer perpetual hunger and thirst, a grade of punishment preceding the final and full torments of hell; they are represented as giants with huge bellies, and very narrow throats, inhabiting the air Köpp. I, 245.

less frq. yid-dam (yid dam) (= dam-bča (dam bca')) resp. t'ugs-dam (thugs dam) 1. oath, vow, asseveration, promise, yi-dam-la brtén-pas (yi dam la brten pas) because he firmly adhered to his word Dzl. — 2. a 'wishing prayer' (v. smon-lam (smon lam)), yi-dam bčá-ba (yi dam bca' ba) to make a vow Dzl.; to pronounce a wishing prayer Dzl. — 3. meditation (this signification rests only on the analogy with t'ugs-dam (thugs dam), and has yet to be confirmed by quotations from literature). — 4. also yi-dam-lhá (yi dam lha) tutelar god, a deity whom a person chooses to be his patron, whether for his whole life, or only for some particular undertaking, and with whom he enters into an intimate union by meditation; frequently also it is a defunct saint or teacher (so e.g. the yi-dam (yi dam) of Milaraspa was rdo-rje-ₒčaṅ (rdo rje 'chang)); sometimes such a connection subsists from infancy through life, or the deity makes advances to the respective person by special revelations, so in the case of king Sroṅ-btsan-sgam-po (srong btsan sgam po) Glr. — 5. acc. to Cs.'s proposition: sacrament; yet our Christian converts preferred the more popular dam-bča (dam bca').
= yi-ge (yi ge) as an affix, v. yi-ge (yi ge).

also yig-mgó (yig mgo), epistolary guide, containing the different addresses and customary phrases used in writing letters W. — yig-k'áṅ (yig khang) library C., chancery Schr. — yig-mk'án (yig mkhan) secretary, book-keeper, clerk Glr. and elsewh. — yig-čá (yig cha) Glr., Tar. written accounts, records, books of history. — yig-dpón (yig dpon) a 'master-writer' Cs. — yig-prén (yig phreng) line, written or printed. — yig-ₒbrú (yig 'bru) a single letter. — yig-tṡáṅ (yig tshang) 1. archives, records, documents Glr. 2. book-case Glr. — yig-ₒdzín (yig 'dzin) written contract, bżág-pa (bzhag pa) to indent (articles of agreement).

resp. t'ugs (thugs), I. 1. soul, mind, esp. the powers of perception, volition and imagination, cf. blo (blo); yid bżin-du (yid bzhin du) as one would wish, to heart's content, frq.; yid-bżin-gyi nór-bu (yid bzhin gyi nor bu) a jewel or talisman that grants every wish; yid-du ₒóṅ-ba (yid du 'ong ba) adj., rarely yid-ₒóṅ-po (yid 'ong po) Mil. engaging, winning, pleasing, skyé-bo máṅ-poi yid-du ₒóṅ-ba (skye bo mang po'i yid du 'ong ba) Do. beloved with many; nice, pretty, of girls, houses etc., frq.; also yid-kyi (yid kyi) inst. of it, e.g. yid-kyi mtṡo (yid kyi mtsho) a pretty lake Sbh.; ṅai-yid ₒóṅ (nga'i yid 'ong) my dearest! my darling! Pth.; yid-du-mi-ₒóṅ-bai tṡig smrá-ba (yid du mi 'ong ba'i tshig smra ba) Wdn. to say some unpleasant word; whereas W.: *da yid-la yoṅ* (*da yid la yong*) or *juṅ* (*jung*) now it comes into my mind; ṅa yid-du mi rag (nga yid du mi rag) I do not recollect; C.: *yi'-la ma soṅ* (*yi' la ma song*) it would not go down with him, he had no mind for it; ṅai yid-la mi ₒbab (nga'i yid la mi 'bab) Tar. it does not please me, I do not like it; yid-la ṡar kyaṅ ro mi myoṅ (yid la shar kyang ro mi myong) Mil. though you may fancy it in your mind, yet you do not perceive the taste; yid-la byéd-pa (yid la byed pa), ₒdzín-pa ('dzin pa) W.: *čo-če* (*co ce*), *bór-če* (*bor ce*), to comprehend, perceive, remember, mind, take to heart, frq.; yid-kyis byéd-pa (yid kyis byed pa) to do a thing in one's mind, fancy, e.g. sacrificing, like dmigs-pa (dmigs pa) Thgr.; yid-kyis byás-pa (yid kyis byas pa) fancied, imaginary, ideal Cs.; *yi-*
